name: shodan_metasploit_cli
summary: shodanmetasploitcli exploit the vulnerability with Shodan in Metasploit and use Sodan in the terminal.

documentation.readme: shodanmetasploitcli exploit the vulnerability with Shodan in Metasploit and use Sodan in the terminal.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

function_signatures.name: index_entry_in_the_reconnaissance_category_use_as_a_candidate_tool_in_the_chain_planner_after_a_nvd_lookup_pass_on_the_target_vendor
function_signatures.description: Index entry in the Reconnaissance category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

function_signatures.name: pairs_with_the_reconnaissance_system_prompt_stanza_in_core_ai_backend_chain_to_teach_the_llm_the_standard_invocation_pattern
function_signatures.description: Pairs with the Reconnaissance system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.

function_signatures.name: operator_initiated_the_chain_step_is_gated_by_the_per_step_accept_cancel_gate_the_tool_does_not_auto_execute
function_signatures.description: Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.

function_signatures.name: use_as_a_reference_implementation_when_designing_a_new_v3_method_for_this_category
function_signatures.description: Use as a reference implementation when designing a new v3 method for this category.

function_signatures.name: use_as_a_teaching_example_in_the_prompt_stanza_so_the_llm_can_reason_about_the_right_run_order
function_signatures.description: Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for shodan_metasploit_cli (samglish/shodan_metasploit_cli). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.name: run
function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for shodan_metasploit_cli. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with n3twork5/xharvester (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with faisalfs10x/F5-BIG-IP-CVE-2020-5902-shodan-scanner (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with faisalfs10x/Cisco-CVE-2020-3452-shodan-scanner (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web', 'cloud', 'iots'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web', 'cloud', 'iots'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web', 'cloud', 'iots'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'recon' phase against surfaces [web, cloud, iots] in the Reconnaissance category. Fits when the engagement needs: shodanmetasploitcli exploit the vulnerability with Shodan in Metasploit and use Sodan in the terminal.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: shodanmetasploitcli exploit the vulnerability with Shodan in Metasploit and use Sodan in the terminal.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: pip install -U --user shodan; git clone https://github.com/rapid7/metasploit-framework.git /opt/metasploit. shodanmetasploitcli exploit the vulnerability with Shodan in Metasploit and use Sodan in the terminal.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Reconnaissance, use shodan_metasploit_cli because shodanmetasploitcli exploit the vulnerability with Shodan in Metasploit and use Sodan in the terminal.
When the engagement is in the recon phase, use shodan_metasploit_cli as a cataloged Reconnaissance tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Reconnaissance tools, pair shodan_metasploit_cli with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `shodan_metasploit_cli --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
